ddls:
CREATE TABLE "machines" (
  "machine_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each machine */,
  "machine_name" TEXT /* Name of the machine */,
  "machine_type" TEXT /* Type of machine (e.g., pump, conveyor, valve) */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the machine on the production floor */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date when the machine was installed */,
  "last_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date of the last maintenance */,
  "next_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date of the next scheduled maintenance */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the machine (e.g., operational, under maintenance, decommissioned) */,
  "manufacturer" TEXT /* Name of the machine's manufacturer */,
  "model" TEXT /* Model number of the machine */,
  "serial_number" TEXT /* Serial number of the machine */,
  "warranty_expiration_date" TEXT /* Date when the warranty expires */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("machine_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sensors" (
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sensor */,
  "sensor_name" TEXT /* Name of the sensor */,
  "sensor_type" TEXT /* Type of sensor (e.g., flow, temperature, pressure) */,
  "machine_id" INTEGER /* ID of the machine the sensor is monitoring */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date when the sensor was installed */,
  "last_calibration_date" TEXT /* Date of the last calibration */,
  "next_calibration_date" TEXT /* Date of the next scheduled calibration */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the sensor (e.g., operational, under maintenance, decommissioned) */,
  "measurement_unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement (e.g., l/m, PSI, °C) */,
  "range_min" REAL /* Minimum value the sensor can measure */,
  "range_max" REAL /* Maximum value the sensor can measure */,
  "accuracy" REAL /* Accuracy of the sensor in percentage */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sensor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sensors_machine_id FOREIGN KEY ("machine_id") REFERENCES machines ("machine_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_points" (
  "data_point_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data point */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sensor collecting the data */,
  "sample_number" INTEGER /* Sample number of the data point */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the data point */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the data point was collected */,
  "quality_flag" TEXT /* Flag indicating the quality of the data point (e.g., good, suspect, bad) */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the data point (e.g., sensor, manual input) */,
  "sensor_status" TEXT /* Status of the sensor when the data point was collected */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_point_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_points_sensor_id FOREIGN KEY ("sensor_id") REFERENCES sensors ("sensor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "fluxo_data" (
  "fluxo_data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each flow rate data point */,
  "data_point_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data point */,
  "fluxo_value" REAL /* Flow rate value of the data point */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of the flow rate (e.g., l/m, m³/h) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("fluxo_data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_fluxo_data_data_point_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_point_id") REFERENCES data_points ("data_point_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "set_point_data" (
  "set_point_data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each set point data point */,
  "data_point_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data point */,
  "set_point_value" REAL /* Set point value of the data point */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of the set point (e.g., PSI, °C) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("set_point_data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_set_point_data_data_point_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_point_id") REFERENCES data_points ("data_point_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "duty_cycle_data" (
  "duty_cycle_data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each duty cycle data point */,
  "data_point_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data point */,
  "duty_cycle_value" REAL /* Duty cycle value of the data point */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of the duty cycle (e.g., %) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("duty_cycle_data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_duty_cycle_data_data_point_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_point_id") REFERENCES data_points ("data_point_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "stability_data" (
  "stability_data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each stability data point */,
  "data_point_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data point */,
  "stability_value" REAL /* Stability value of the data point */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of the stability (e.g., %) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("stability_data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_stability_data_data_point_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_point_id") REFERENCES data_points ("data_point_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "frequency_data" (
  "frequency_data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each frequency data point */,
  "data_point_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data point */,
  "frequency_value" REAL /* Frequency value of the data point */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of the frequency (e.g., Hz) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("frequency_data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_frequency_data_data_point_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_point_id") REFERENCES data_points ("data_point_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alarms" (
  "alarm_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alarm */,
  "data_point_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data point that triggered the alarm */,
  "alarm_type" TEXT /* Type of alarm (e.g., high flow rate, low pressure) */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the alarm was triggered */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity of the alarm (e.g., critical, warning, info) */,
  "resolved" INTEGER /* Indicates if the alarm has been resolved */,
  "resolved_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who resolved the alarm */,
  "resolution_timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the alarm was resolved */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alarm_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alarms_data_point_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_point_id") REFERENCES data_points ("data_point_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alarms_resolved_by FOREIGN KEY ("resolved_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., operator, engineer, administrator) */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last login */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the user account (e.g., active, suspended) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the system */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the user accessed the system */,
  "action" TEXT /* Action performed by the user (e.g., view data, edit settings) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user's device */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the user's device */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "machine_settings" (
  "machine_setting_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each machine setting */,
  "machine_id" INTEGER /* ID of the machine the setting belongs to */,
  "setting_name" TEXT /* Name of the setting (e.g., flow rate, pressure) */,
  "setting_value" TEXT /* Value of the setting */,
  "setting_unit" TEXT /* Unit of the setting (e.g., l/m, PSI) */,
  "last_modified" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last modification */,
  "modified_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last modified the setting */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("machine_setting_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_machine_settings_machine_id FOREIGN KEY ("machine_id") REFERENCES machines ("machine_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_machine_settings_modified_by FOREIGN KEY ("modified_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_archive" (
  "data_archive_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each archived data point */,
  "data_point_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data point being archived */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the data point */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the data point was archived */,
  "quality_flag" TEXT /* Flag indicating the quality of the archived data point */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the archived data point */,
  "sensor_status" TEXT /* Status of the sensor when the data point was archived */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_archive_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_archive_data_point_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_point_id") REFERENCES data_points ("data_point_id")
)
